quote_text: Linguistic leave is a special form of leave granted to employees of all nationalities who wish to learn Luxembourgish or to improve their knowledge of the language.

The following people are eligible for linguistic leave: employees ... who have-at the time of application-at least 6 months of service with the same employer; ... self-employed activity or a liberal profession in Luxembourg for at least 6 months already.

The Luxemburgish language courses eligible for linguistic leave are those provided in Luxembourg or abroad by: public or private teaching establishments recognised by the public authorities ... foundations, natural persons or private associations specifically approved by the Minister responsible for vocational training ... Language courses which only form a part of a complete vocational training programme or project ... are not eligible.
The total duration of the linguistic leave is limited to 200 hours, which must be divided into 2 stages of 80 to 120 hours each for each beneficiary throughout their professional career.
Joining the 'Biergerpakt' is voluntary and gives access to the intercultural living together programme. ... Any person of legal age who lives or works in Luxembourg.

The programme consists of: 3 introductory modules to life in Luxembourg ... one or more modules leading to at least introductory level A.1.1 ... in at least one of Luxembourg's 3 administrative languages (Luxembourgish, German and French).

advanced modules ... aimed at: language learning and practice; understanding administrative procedures; deepening knowledge of life in Luxembourg, its history and cultural heritage; promoting diversity and interculturality

The Luxembourg State supports businesses in their vocational training programmes by contributing to the funding of their training plans. ... a business may apply for State co-funding of its training activities. ... This aid does not apply to mandatory continuing training that is legally required to practise a regulated profession.
Any private sector business legally established in Luxembourg and carrying out most of its activities in the country can claim financial aid from the State.

Businesses may receive 15 % of the annual amount invested in training in financial support for training (taxable). ... The State's financial contribution is increased to 35% (taxable) for the wage costs of participants who satisfy the following criteria
Examples of training courses: language courses; ... job-induction training (new employees, job transfers).

The Institut national des langues Luxembourg is a public centre for adult learning and certification of modern language skills. ... Luxembourg ... hosts a wide variety of expatriates and a large number of cross-border workers.

The INLL is a public centre for modern language learning and certification, committed to promoting plurilingualism, facilitating social integration, contributing to people's employability, facilitating intercultural exchange through languages and training staff. ... The INLL offers courses for adults in 13 languages:
